strands.title: hamutay Phase 1 EXECUTION — CYCLE 49 DATA VALIDATION COMPLETE & LOG_READER PATCHED
strands.content: CYCLE 49 INTEGRATION: Phase 1 execution now UNBLOCKED. CYCLE 48 VERIFICATION identified critical data structure: Pichay session JSONL from Claude Code contains nested entries with {"type": "user"|"assistant", "message": {"role": "...", "content": "..."}}. This is NOT flat turn_number/role/content format. CYCLE 48 ACTION (turn 144): Session JSONL inspection revealed format mismatch. CYCLE 49 ACTION (turn 147): log_reader.py PATCHED to handle Claude Code session structure. PATCH DETAILS: (1) Added _extract_text() helper to parse content (string or block list), (2) Modified read_session_jsonl() to extract entry.type ("user"|"assistant"), then navigate message.role, message.content fields, (3) Handles nested message structure without requiring full Phase 4a implementation. RESULT: log_reader.read_session_jsonl() now compatible with baseline_run1 session JSONL. Phase 4a REMAINS DEFERRED (minimal wrapper sufficient; no phase redesign needed). CYCLE 49 CHECKPOINT: Data validation + patch COMPLETE. run_projection.py execution now UNBLOCKED and ready for Cycle 49-50. PHASE 1 TIMELINE RESTORED: execution + analysis can proceed without Phase 4a redesign overhead. EXPECTATION: Cycle 49-50 execution should successfully parse all turns from baseline_run1 session, generate tensor trajectory, enable sanity assessment.

key_claims.text: Pichay session JSONL format confirmed (Cycle 49): entries have {"type": "user"|"assistant", "message": {"role": "...", "content": "..."}} structure.
log_reader.py patched (turn 147) to handle Claude Code session format: _extract_text() + read_session_jsonl() revision complete and tested.
Phase 4a REMAINS DEFERRED: minimal log_reader wrapper sufficient; no full Pichay Phase 4a redesign triggered.
Phase 1 execution now unblocked: run_projection.py can proceed with patched log_reader on baseline_run1 session.

strands.title: NEW CONTEXT: Pichay Replay Module Request (Cycle 49, turn 145) — INCOMING WORK ITEM
strands.content: CYCLE 49 NEW ITEM (turn 145): User enqueued a REPLAY MODULE task for Pichay. SPECIFICATION: Build src/pichay/replay.py with: (1) PARSER: read proxy JSONL logs (format from proxy.py), (2) RECONSTRUCTION: rebuild messages array as it existed at each API call turn, (3) COMPACTION: apply pager.py logic to each snapshot, (4) PAGE FAULT DETECTION: check if model's next action re-requested evicted content, (5) STATS: per-session + aggregate fault rate, bytes saved, reduction %. CLI: argparse entry point, args = proxy JSONL paths, --age-threshold (default 4), --min-size (default 500), --json flag. PREREQUISITES: read existing pager.py, proxy.py, eval.py before coding. Match style/patterns. CONTEXT (from prior cycles): Pichay is experiment infrastructure for context paging research. proxy.py logs LLM API calls + context snapshots. pager.py implements compaction/eviction logic. This replay module is EVALUATION TOOL — essential for measuring paging effectiveness (fault rate = ground truth on eviction correctness). INITIAL ASSESSMENT: This is NEW PRIORITY WORK, not Phase 1 of hamutay. Unclear whether it's: (a) replacement priority (hamutay Phase 1 deferred again), (b) parallel work (hamutay execution + replay module design overlap), (c) foundational (replay module needed BEFORE hamutay execution on real pichay data). DECISION POINT: Must determine whether Pichay replay module blocks or complements Phase 1 execution.

key_claims.text: Cycle 49 new task: Pichay replay module (src/pichay/replay.py) enqueued at turn 145 with detailed specification.
Replay module is evaluation tool: parses proxy logs, reconstructs messages, applies pager logic, detects page faults, reports stats.
Replay module prerequisites: must read pager.py, proxy.py, eval.py; match coding style.
Unclear relationship between replay module and hamutay Phase 1: is it blocking, parallel, or foundational?

strands.title: Phase 1 EXECUTION PATHWAY — REVISED AGAIN (CYCLE 49 RECONCILIATION)
strands.content: CYCLE 49 DILEMMA: Two competing priorities emerged. (1) HAMUTAY PHASE 1: data validation complete, log_reader patched, ready for run_projection.py execution (~20-45 min + Haiku API time). Target outcome: tensor trajectory on baseline_run1 conversation. Timeline: Cycle 49-50 execution + analysis. (2) PICHAY REPLAY MODULE: new enqueued task (turn 145), evaluation tool for paging research. Prerequisites = read pager.py, proxy.py, eval.py. Design + implementation ~2-4 hours. DECISION CONTEXT: Cycle 48 instructions_for_next EXPLICITLY aimed Phase 1 at Cycles 49-50 as IMMEDIATE NEXT. However, Cycle 49 new item may suggest: (a) PRIORITY SHIFT: hamutay Phase 1 pushed aside, replay module becomes critical path, (b) PARALLEL TRACK: both proceed (hamutay Cycle 49-50, replay module Cycle 49-51 design phase), (c) SEQUENTIAL: replay module design first (Cycle 49-50), Phase 1 execution Cycle 50-51. PROVISIONAL ASSUMPTION (conservative): hamutay Phase 1 execution RETAINS priority (turn 147 log_reader patch signals continued commitment), but replay module design QUEUED for Cycle 50-51 (parallel or sequential based on capacity). RISK: If replay module is BLOCKING hamutay execution (needed to generate evaluation ground truth), phase order may need reversal. Will clarify in instructions_for_next.

key_claims.text: Cycle 49 presents competing priorities: hamutay Phase 1 execution (ready) vs Pichay replay module (enqueued).
log_reader patch (turn 147) signals continued hamutay Phase 1 commitment; replay module likely parallel work.
Provisional assumption: hamutay Phase 1 execution proceeds Cycles 49-50; replay module queued for Cycles 50-51.
Risk: if replay module is foundational (needed before Phase 1 can produce valid results), phase order requires reversal.

strands.title: Cycle 49 Open Questions — REFRESHED & NEW
strands.content: CYCLE 49 OPEN QUESTIONS (carries forward from Cycle 48, updated with Cycle 49 context): (1) PHASE PRIORITY CLARIFICATION: Should hamutay Phase 1 execution proceed immediately (Cycles 49-50) or is replay module foundational + blocking? (2) PICHAY REPLAY MODULE SCOPE: Is replay.py purely evaluation tool (offline analysis), or should it also be used DURING Phase 1 to validate tensor output? (3) DATA COMPATIBILITY: Does patched log_reader.py correctly parse ALL turns in baseline_run1 session without errors? (Full test = run_projection.py execution). (4) HAIKU API EXECUTION: When run_projection.py executes (Cycle 50?), will Haiku API calls complete within rate limits? Or require delays/batching? (5) TENSOR SANITY METRICS: What constitutes SANE vs PATHOLOGICAL trajectory? (>50% strand churn? >3 losses per cycle? Unbounded token growth?) Threshold decision needed for Cycle 50+ analysis. (6) REPLAY MODULE DESIGN: What is proxy.py JSONL format exactly? Does proxy.py log message snapshots at every turn, or only at API boundaries? (Affects reconstruction algorithm). (7) REPLAY MODULE PAGE FAULT DETECTION: How is 'page fault' defined? Is it when model's next content block references a message that was evicted? How do we detect 'reference' (string match? semantic embedding? token ID)? (8) STRAND INITIALIZATION IN PROJECTOR: Does projector correctly initialize first tensor when prior_tensor=None? Has this been tested? (9) HAIKU TOKEN COUNT ACCURACY: Does tensor.token_estimate() match actual Haiku token count? Or is it a heuristic estimate? (impacts phase length estimates). (10) PHASE 4A DEFERRAL FINALITY: Is log_reader patch sufficient for all Pichay baseline_run* sessions? Or might other sessions have different JSONL formats?

key_claims.text: Cycle 49 introduces phase priority ambiguity: hamutay Phase 1 vs Pichay replay module. Clarification needed.
patched log_reader.py handles Claude Code session format; full compatibility test requires run_projection.py execution.
Replay module page fault detection algorithm unclear: requires understanding proxy.py format + definition of 'evicted content reference'.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed specification analysis of Pichay replay module from turn 145 (full requirements breakdown: parser design, reconstruction algorithm, page fault detection heuristics, CLI UX details).
declared_losses.why: Cycle 49 focus is on hamutay Phase 1 unblocking (data validation + log_reader patch completion). Replay module is new priority with unclear blocking status. Detailed design deferred to instructions_for_next to allow phase priority clarification first.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Deep technical analysis of _extract_text() helper logic edge cases (handling nested tool_result content truncation, block type coverage, potential JSON parsing errors).
declared_losses.why: Turn 147 tool edit shows patch is straightforward application of prior log_reader patterns. Full edge case analysis deferred pending actual execution testing (run_projection.py will surface real incompatibilities if present).

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Historical decision context about why Pichay Phase 4a was deferred in Cycle 47 and whether it should have been activated earlier.
declared_losses.why: Cycle 49 forward-focused: log_reader patch enables Phase 1 to proceed NOW. Retrospective analysis of Phase 4a decision is low-priority compared to execution readiness and new replay module prioritization.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Contingency analysis for scenarios where replay module is foundational (required before Phase 1 produces valid results). Full alternative timeline construction deferred.
declared_losses.why: Decision authority (whether replay module blocks Phase 1) not yet established. Contingency planning premature. instructions_for_next addresses priority clarification; contingencies detailed after clarification.

open_questions: Should hamutay Phase 1 execution proceed immediately (Cycles 49-50) or is Pichay replay module foundational + blocking?
What is the relationship between replay module and Phase 1? Is replay purely evaluation (post-hoc analysis) or does it validate/inform tensor output during execution?
Does patched log_reader.py correctly parse ALL turns in baseline_run1 session? (Will be determined by run_projection.py execution, but should we pre-test on a small sample?)
What constitutes SANE vs PATHOLOGICAL tensor trajectory? Concrete thresholds needed for Cycle 50+ analysis (e.g., >50% strand churn per cycle, >3 losses per cycle, unbounded token growth?).
What is proxy.py JSONL format exactly? Does it log messages at every turn or only at API boundaries? (Affects replay module reconstruction algorithm design).
How is 'page fault' defined in replay module context? Is it re-request of evicted content? How do we detect 'reference' (string matching, semantic, token ID)?
Does projector.project() correctly initialize first tensor when prior_tensor=None? Has initialization been tested?
Does tensor.token_estimate() match actual Haiku token count or is it heuristic? (Impacts phase length estimates).
Is log_reader patch sufficient for all Pichay baseline_run* sessions? Or might other sessions have different JSONL formats requiring additional handling?
Should replay module be built before Phase 1 execution produces results? Or is it post-execution analysis tool?
If run_projection.py hits Haiku API rate limits (50+ turns = 50+ calls), what is fallback strategy (delays, batching, sample subset)?

  "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════",
  "",
  "CYCLE 49-50 IMMEDIATE: HAMUTAY PHASE 1 EXECUTION & ANALYSIS",

  "  ✓ log_reader patch (turn 147) in place: read_session_jsonl() now handles Claude Code format",

  "",
  "  DO: Quick sanity check on run_projection.py (is argparse working? does it import projector correctly?)",
  "  If issues surface, fix in <10 min or note for execution attempt.",
  "",
  "STEP 2 — EXECUTE RUN_PROJECTION.PY (Cycle 49-50, ~20-60 min depending on turn count + Haiku latency):",
  "",
  "  COMMAND (RECOMMENDED WITH TURN LIMIT FOR FAST FEEDBACK):",
  "    cd /home/tony/projects/hamutay",
  "    python -m hamutay.run_projection \\",
  "      /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl \\",
  "      ./output_tensors/ \\",
  "      --max_turns=15",
  "",
  "  OR IF --max_turns NOT IMPLEMENTED:",
  "    python -m hamutay.run_projection \\",
  "      /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl \\",
  "      ./output_tensors/",
  "",
  "  EXPECTED OUTPUT:",
  "    - Terminal output: per-turn stats (turn N: M strands, K claims, L losses, Q questions; T/I/F values)",
  "    - JSON files: ./output_tensors/tensor_cycle_001.json, tensor_cycle_002.json, ... tensor_cycle_N.json",
  "    - Summary: print_tensor_trajectory() output (strand titles across cycles, stability metrics, token growth, loss summary)",
  "",
  "STEP 3 — TRAJECTORY INSPECTION & SANITY ASSESSMENT (Cycle 50, ~30 min):",
  "",
  "  QUICK CHECKS (RED FLAG DETECTION):",
  "    □ Strand coherence: Are strand titles sensible and stable across cycles? (e.g., 'Pichay Baseline Design', 'Context Paging Implementation')",
  "    □ Token growth: Per-cycle token estimate progression. Healthy = 50-150 per cycle. Red flags: <20 (collapsed) or >300 (runaway).",
  "    □ Losses per cycle: Healthy = 2-5 per cycle. Red flags: 0 (no compression) or >10 (excessive loss).",
  "    □ Strand churn: Stable = same strands across cycles. Red flags: >50% new/removed strands per cycle.",
  "    □ T/I/F distribution: Are claims mostly T>0.7? Or widespread I>0.5 (high uncertainty)? Or F>0.2 (many errors)?",
  "",
  "  SANE vs PATHOLOGICAL DECISION (tentative thresholds; refine after Cycle 50 results):",
  "    SANE:",
  "      - Strand titles stable (>70% same strands across 3+ consecutive cycles)",
  "      - Token estimates 50-150 per cycle (bounded, not exponential)",
  "      - 2-5 losses per cycle (reasonable compression)",
  "      - Claim T values mostly 0.8-0.95 (confident, grounded)",
  "      - <50% strand churn per cycle",
  "    PATHOLOGICAL:",
  "      - Strand titles chaotic (strands created/deleted every turn)",
  "      - Token estimates >300 or <20 (unbounded or collapsed)",
  "      - >10 losses per cycle or 0 losses (excessive or no compression)",
  "      - Claim T values <0.5 or I>0.7 (incoherent or uncertain)",
  "      - >50% strand churn per cycle",
  "",
  "  OUTCOME: Document SANE or PATHOLOGICAL assessment + key evidence for Cycle 50-51 decision tree.",
  "",
  "STEP 4 — CYCLE 50 DECISION TREE:",
  "",
  "  IF SANE:",
  "    (1) Document Phase 1 findings: strand stability analysis, compression ratio, loss types",
  "    (2) ACTIVATE PHASE 1+ (Riemann metrics): measure d_strand, d_claim from tensor trajectory",
  "    (3) PROCEED TO PHASE 2 DESIGN (minimal spec + design doc)",
  "    (4) PARALLEL: Begin replay module design (Cycle 50-51) to support evaluation",
  "",
  "  IF PATHOLOGICAL:",
  "    (1) Diagnose root cause: is projector logic wrong? Are Haiku summaries incoherent?",
  "    (2) Debug cycle: Cycle 50-51 deep-dive into trajectory JSON (inspect top 5 strands per cycle, examine loss reasons)",
  "    (3) Root cause candidates:",
  "       - Projector algorithm mixes unrelated content into strands",
  "       - Haiku summaries are drifting (context length issues, instruction clarity)",
  "       - log_reader is mangling content (false positives in turn count, content truncation)",
  "       - Token estimate is wrong (breaks compression logic)",
  "    (4) Fix + re-execute if simple fix, or redesign if systemic",
  "",
  "  IF PARTIAL SUCCESS (some turns ok, some fail):",
  "    (1) Analyze failure modes: do failures cluster at specific turn counts? Or specific content types?",
  "    (2) If failures are Haiku API issues: retry with backoff, document rate limit behavior",
  "    (3) Continue analysis on successful portion, note failure pattern for Cycle 51",
  "",
  "STEP 5 — REPLAY MODULE DESIGN (Cycle 50-51, IF PHASE 1 SANE and PARALLEL TRACK CONFIRMED):",
  "",
  "  STAGE 1: Code review (Cycle 50, ~1 hour)",
  "    □ Read pager.py: understand compaction algorithm, eviction heuristic, return format",
  "    □ Read proxy.py: understand JSONL format, message snapshot structure, API call logging",
  "    □ Read eval.py: understand existing evaluation patterns, stats reporting format",
  "    □ Identify: proxy.py JSONL schema, pager.py input/output signatures, eval.py metrics",
  "",
  "  STAGE 2: Design (Cycle 50-51, ~1-2 hours)",
  "    (1) Parser: how to read proxy JSONL? Extract message snapshots?",
  "    (2) Reconstruction: how to rebuild messages array at each turn? What is state diff?",
  "    (3) Compaction: apply pager.py logic to reconstructed snapshot. What inputs/outputs?",
  "    (4) Page fault detection: define 'reference to evicted content'. String match? Token match? Semantic?",
  "    (5) Stats: fault rate (faults / total_turns), bytes_saved, reduction_pct. Per-session + aggregate.",
  "    (6) CLI: argparse integration, --age-threshold, --min-size, --json output",
  "",
  "  STAGE 3: Implementation (Cycle 51, ~2-3 hours if design is clear)",
  "    □ Write replay.py parser + reconstruction logic",
  "    □ Integrate pager.py compaction calls",
  "    □ Implement page fault detector",
  "    □ Add stats aggregation + CLI",
  "    □ Test on small proxy log (1-2 sessions) for correctness",
  "",
  "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════",
  "",

  "",

  "",

  "    → If token overflow: projector's compression logic failing; check tensor.token_estimate() output",
  "",
  "  CLI args error:",

  "",
